title: blumea laevis var. minor
intro: Blumea laevis var. minor, also known as White Paperflower, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae), native to East Asia and known for its small, white flowers and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Blumea laevis var. minor include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
